tools.name: mg_list
tools.description: List all available Marketgenius tools with their names, descriptions, categories, and resource URIs. Use mg_list to discover what's available, or filter by category.

tools.name: mg_calculator_cagr
tools.description:
Open the CAGR Calculator React MCP App UI.
ALWAYS ask first — never call this tool silently:
"Open the calculator directly, or pre-fill it with your inputs?"

IF directly → call this tool now with no params. STOP. Do not ask anything else.
IF pre-fill → collect only the input schema fields — do not

tools.name: mg_calculator_cagr_update
tools.description:
Update one or more fields in the already-open CAGR Calculator.
ALWAYS call mg_calculator_cagr_state first — the user may have
changed values manually in the GUI and you must not overwrite them.
Pass the version you received from that call (or from the open tool
if updating immediately after opening)

tools.name: mg_calculator_cagr_sync
tools.description:
Sync user-entered field values of the open CAGR Calculator back
to the session store so the model can read them via the state tool.
Called by the View after any field change; hidden from the model.

tools.name: mg_calculator_cagr_state
tools.description:
Read the current field values of the open CAGR Calculator.
Call this before answering any question about the current values —
the user may have changed fields manually in the GUI.
Do not call this on a loop; call it once on demand.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "mg_calculator_cagr" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Open the CAGR Calculator React MCP App UI. ALWAYS ask first — never call this tool silently: "Open the calculator directly, or pre-fill it with your inputs?" IF…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "mg_calculator_cagr" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Open the CAGR Calculator React MCP App UI. ALWAYS ask first — never call this tool silently: "Open the calculator directly, or pre-fill it with your inputs?" IF…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "mg_calculator_cagr_update" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Update one or more fields in the already-open CAGR Calculator. ALWAYS call mg_calculator_cagr_state first — the user may have changed values manually in the GUI…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.
